summary: Advanced vi0102 decompilation by adding typed setup logic, archive/camera/fog/light data structures, concrete function prototypes, and a VI-specific naming convention. The PR also globally renamed several baselib GObj display callbacks from address-based names to semantic names, requiring many assembly and symbols.txt updates. The author stated vi0102 was mostly matched, with remaining reallocations and register swaps.
what_changed: Implemented substantial vi0102 C logic where stubs or address-named functions had existed, including vi0102_8031CB00, vi0102_JObjCallback, vi0102_CameraCallback, vi0102_RunFrame, vi0102_Initialize, vi0102_8031D000, and vi0102_8031D020.
Added local vi0102 archive data structs for joints, camera animations, camera data, fog data, and scene data, tying Vi0102.dat / visual0102Scene archive contents to typed fields.

vi0102_Initialize now sets up audio/effects/archive loading, creates camera, joint, fog, and light GObjs, installs GX links/procs, requests/steps animations, and pulls the fog color into the screen erase color.

vi0102_8031CB00 now performs scene/player setup for Mario and Luigi using camera, collision, ground/stage, item, ftDemo, player, and audio calls, with costume arguments passed through from un_804D6F60_t fields.
Updated vi0102.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes to typed prototypes and converted un_804D6F60_t into a typedef with explicit s8 fields unk_0 through unk_3 plus padding, preserving size 0x8.
Renamed baselib GObj callbacks globally: HSD_GObj_80391044 to HSD_GObj_LObjCallback, HSD_GObj_80391070 to HSD_GObj_JObjCallback, and HSD_GObj_803910B4 to HSD_GObj_FogCallback.
Propagated the GObj callback renames through many asm files, source files, and config/GALE01/symbols.txt so references and symbol names remained consistent.
Changed VI camera erase-color handling toward GXColor: vi0102 and vi0501 store erase colors as GXColor and pass them to vi_RunCamera via a u8 pointer cast.
Updated vi0501 camera callback prototype to accept an unused int argument, matching the GObj/GX callback style used by GObj_SetupGXLinkMax.
Improved at least one helper prototype needed by vi0102 work: gm_8016895C was changed from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void gm_8016895C(HSD_JObj*, void*, s32).
smart_moves: The global rename of HSD_GObj_* address symbols to HSD_GObj_JObjCallback, HSD_GObj_LObjCallback, and HSD_GObj_FogCallback converted high-traffic callbacks into reusable semantic API names, making future display-list/GX-link matching easier across many systems.
Using scene-local archive structs let vi0102_Initialize express the asset layout directly: jointdata, camdata, lightdata, and fogdata are accessed through typed fields rather than raw offsets.
The PR separated callbacks by role: vi0102_JObjCallback advances JObj animation, vi0102_CameraCallback handles shadow/camera rendering, and baselib callbacks handle JObj/LObj/Fog display setup.
Keeping STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(un_804D6F60_t) == 0x8) while refining fields protected ABI/layout assumptions during prototype cleanup.
Updating symbols.txt and asm labels together avoided drift between C names, assembly references, and linker symbols after broad callback renames.
Adding/includes and prototypes for the real callees converted unknown calls into typed dependencies, which is useful groundwork even when the function is not yet perfectly matched.
decomp_lessons: For VI scene work, use a module-specific prefix such as vi0102_ for newly identified functions, and prefer semantic callback suffixes like CameraCallback, JObjCallback, RunFrame, and Initialize when the role is known.

Address-based baselib callbacks can often be identified by their object type behavior: JObj callbacks call HSD_JObjDispAll, LObj callbacks operate on HSD_LObj and current CObj, and fog callbacks call HSD_FogSet.
When a visual scene loads a .dat archive, model the archive root with small typed structs first; this can make camera, joint, fog, and light setup code much more matchable and reviewable.
GX callback signatures may include unused parameters; adding the unused int to camera/fog/light/JObj callback prototypes can match the expected GObj_SetupGXLink calling convention.
Represent erase colors as GXColor when the source data comes from fog->color, but be prepared to cast to u8* if shared helpers still take byte-array color parameters.

A mostly matched function may still retain reallocations/regswaps; the PR explicitly reports vi0102 as mostly matched rather than fully clean, so future work should inspect register allocation before treating it as final.
naming_conventions: VI scene functions use a scene prefix: vi0102_8031CB00, vi0102_Initialize, vi0102_RunFrame, vi0102_CameraCallback, vi0102_JObjCallback.
Known baselib GObj callbacks should use object-role names instead of address-only names: HSD_GObj_JObjCallback, HSD_GObj_LObjCallback, HSD_GObj_FogCallback.
Archive helper structs in vi0102.c use a module prefix plus data role, such as vi0102_archivejoints, vi0102_camanims, vi0102_archivecamdata, vi0102_archivefogdata, and vi0102_data.
When a function role is not fully known, the PR still retains address-bearing names under the module prefix, e.g. vi0102_8031CB00 and vi0102_8031D020, rather than bare vi_8031CB00.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Propagate semantic symbol renames through asm references by replacing @ha/@l pairs and direct bl targets, e.g. HSD_GObj_80391070@ha/@l to HSD_GObj_JObjCallback@ha/@l.
Keep symbols.txt in sync with source and asm renames so address-preserving name changes do not break generated assembly references.
Use typed prototypes for callees such as gm_8016895C(HSD_JObj*, void*, s32) to reduce UNK_PARAMS noise and make callsites in matched C express the expected argument registers.
Use PAD_STACK where needed in callbacks such as vi0102_CameraCallback and vi0501 camera callback to preserve stack/matching behavior.
Use #pragma dont_inline around HSD_GObj_JObjCallback internals to preserve matching around HSD_GObj_80390EB8, as shown by the retained TODO about file-boundary/inline behavior.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only explicit quality note is from the PR body: vi0102 was mostly matched, but still had some reallocations and register swaps.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching vi0102 for resolution of the remaining reallocations and register swaps.
Search for other VI modules still using vi_8031-style names that can adopt the viXXXX_ naming convention.
Search for remaining HSD_GObj_80391044, HSD_GObj_80391070, or HSD_GObj_803910B4 references to ensure the semantic callback rename fully propagated.
Compare vi0102 archive struct layout against other visual scene archives to standardize camera/joint/fog/light data typing.
Review vi_RunCamera and erase-color users to see whether its color parameter should eventually become GXColor* instead of u8*.
